beats.narration: Radiography runs on numbers: a unit for radiation present at a point, and a constant for what your source puts out.

payload.title: EXPOSURE & OUTPUT
payload.sub: The numbers every shot is planned on.

beats.narration: Exposure is photons ionizing air: the charge liberated per kilogram of dry air, the coulomb per kilogram. The roentgen is its exact fraction.

payload.heading: Exposure: charge per kilogram of air

beats.narration: Emissivity at one meter: iridium one ninety-two gives about half a roentgen per hour per curie. Cobalt sixty, one point three. Selenium seventy-five, point two.
X ray output is set by its controls: milliamperes scale intensity directly, kilovoltage raises it roughly with its square. Consult the machine's emission chart.

payload.heading: X-ray output is set by the controls

beats.narration: Plan with the constants. The field that exists is the one on a calibrated survey meter. The meter is the fact.

payload.sub: Plan with the constants — believe the meter.
